PICNIC At MATAToRTuN PACK,
WITH MUSIC, SINGING, SPEAK
mo DANCING AND SOME
THING TO EAT"

pages.raw_text:
Bring something to eat! That's
the main thing; something to eat"
So said ]. M. Grimmer when asked
this morning what is the object of
the pioneers picnic.


The Spokane Pioneer society will
hold its eighth annual picnic Thurs
day, June Is. at Natatorium park.
At 3 o'clock the tables will be filled
with something to eat" After the
luncheon there will be a program in
which the pioneers will take part.
Judge L. H. Prather is president of
the society. He will open the pro-
gram with a short address. Jesse
Buchholz will sing. Miss Hazel Grim
mer will recite. Miss Bertha Hollway
will sing solo. after which Judge
L. B. Nash will deliver a Commute
address. Miss Rose Prather will sing
a solo.


A. G. Ansell will deliver a 10-min-
ute memorial address. During the
past year some of the pioneers have
died and Mr.. Ansell will pay trib-
ute to them. The last number of
the program is entitled "Remin-
iscences." They will be limited to 10
minutes each. On the program J. M.
Grimmer. John T. Davie, H. T. Cow
ley "and others" are down for tales
from the old and forgotten past. If
the members bring enough to eat
and manage to get the speakers in
good spirits the tales should be more
than interesting.


After the program the afternoon
will be spent in the park. At 6
O'Clock the picnickers will commence
dancing at the pavilion. The dancing
will last until 10 o'clock.


Who Are Pioneers.


Mr. Grimmer was asked what made
a man a pioneer.


"A man is a pioneer who was here
in November, 1884," said he. 1T was
at that time that the first city char
ter for Spokane Falls was adopted"


The pioneers met last Thursday
evening and elected the following Of
ficers for the coming year: Presi-
dent. L. H. Prather; vice president,
M. M. Herman; treasurer, ]. M. Grim
mer; secretary. 8. L. Alexander.


"The pioneers meet but once or
twice year." said Mr.. Grimmer. I'VE
meet to keep alive the memory of old
times. Our annual picnic is the main
gathering Of our society"


The society was formed eight years
ago. 1t was due to the efforts of A.
J. Ross that the first annual picnic
was held. He was the moving spirit
behind the society.


1t is expected that between 200
and 500 people will attend the picnic.
A general good time is expected If
everyone follows Mr.. Grimmer's ad-
vice and brings something to eat"
